description: Create a GitLab CI pipeline for a Rust project using the i386/rust:1 and amd64/rust:1 Docker images. Define a global before_script that runs getconf LONG_BIT, rustup self‑update, rustup --version, optionally installs beta toolchains for the HOST, adds rustfmt and clippy, copies cached registry files into $CARGO_HOME, and cleans up source directories; and a global after_script that removes unused .crate files, restores the cargo cache, and deletes temporary cargo directories. Add three test jobs—i686-gnulinux, x86_64-gnulinux, and x86_64-i686-gnulinux—each with a cache on cargo/, limited to the "ci" pipeline, that run cargo +beta-$HOST test (with and without --release), cargo +beta-$HOST fmt -- --check, cargo +beta-$HOST clippy --all-targets, delete Cargo.lock, then repeat the tests with the MSRV toolchain; the cross‑compile job also installs gcc‑multilib, adds the i686‑unknown‑linux‑gnu target before testing. Add a pages job that runs only on the "www" pipeline, uses amd64/rust:1, installs the nightly toolchain, clears and creates a public directory, copies static files, runs cargo +$TOOLCHAIN doc --no-deps -Z rustdoc-map, moves the generated docs to public/dev, and publishes the public/ folder as a GitLab Pages artifact. Use the global MSRV variable set to 1.65.0 and per‑job HOST variables (i686, x86_64).
